design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore temple worlds, defeat encounters, collect arks, learn spells/techniques through leveling, recruit partner figurines, progress to next world
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigation across multiple self-contained worlds while building power through experience accumulation to face final boss
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Island mystery solver—awaken on unknown temple island and uncover its secrets by traveling through different worlds, each with distinct stories and characters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Anthology structure: each world has its own character-driven narrative (witch, grappler, swordmaster, ogre, priestess, ninja) loosely tied together by temple framing device. Unusual partner recruitment system via figurines creates asymmetric party composition choices

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Partner selection from available figurines provides tactical customization; experience-based progression allows grinding for stat advantages

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling unlocks new spells and techniques at certain thresholds; two mandatory ark acquisitions (Power Ark, Wisdom Ark) gate partner recruitment and progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Arks found in each world unlock access to subsequent worlds; collection of all arks opens final temple area
replayability_hooks: Two playable protagonists with different narratives (Remeer or Ferris)
Partner figurine selection creates different party compositions per playthrough
Anthology world structure allows varied narrative experiences depending on exploration order

world_building_techniques: Character-specific world storylines (Miriene witch, Reeshine grappler, Lux swordmaster, Kamiwoo ogre, Meisia priestess, Tokio ninja)
Temple framing device ties disparate narratives together
Dual protagonist selection (Remeer/Ferris) creates narrative branching
Figurine recruitment hints at character arcs and relationships

boss_design.philosophy: World-specific antagonist encounters tied to character narratives; final boss represents culmination of temple mystery
notable_bosses: Final boss (unspecified, guards temple exit after ark collection)

faction_indicators: Six world-specific character factions (witch, grappler, swordmaster, ogre, priestess, ninja)
Partner figurine selection creates implicit allegiance to recruited character narratives
danger_signals: Temple island setting with mysterious awakening suggests trapped/cursed scenario
Multiple worlds within temple imply otherworldly or supernatural danger
environmental_storytelling: Temple architecture contains hidden worlds accessible through ark collection
Each world distinctly represents its associated character's domain/narrative
Figurine statues in temple connect character arcs to protagonist's journey

sources_used: wikipedia - Mystic Ark article (gameplay, plot, narrative structure, characters, reception)
